title: ChatGPT vs Grok. Which one actually worked better for you in trading?
author: Helpful-Cut-4903
selftext: I believe many of you have already tested ChatGPT and Grok for trading ideas, signals, or analysis. Curious which one gave you better results?

comments.author: Holiday-Ad-8921
comments.body: imo all the llms are bad for trading because they dont have real time data for trading. but this problem is now solved by @ datamonkey\_bot on telegram which gives real time data with all the indicators in there.  so now that you have the whole biography of that stock with you any reasoning model works very well, for me gemini 2.5 pro has worked best because it has larger context limit

replies.author: StratPIlotAI-GPT
replies.body:
This is exactly why I built [StratPilot AI](https://stratpilotai.com)

It’s hooked up to Perplexity and ChatGPT and then is fed Live data from popular data endpoints in realtime. 

It’s built more so for options strategies, has complex logic built in to structuring options spreads. 

But this works very well for stock direction because after all the bullish or bearish sentiment is one of the inputs to the AI Recommendations. It forecasts moves on a 24-48 hour window. And has confidence scores, win rates projections, risk levels.

It has a real time news scanner as well.

Been building this all year and is finally in production.

Definitely recommend you check it out.

replies.author: Mr_E_T
replies.body: I use rallies.ai. Is linked up with live options data and dark pool data. Also spits out gamma, fibs, etc.

comments.author: Akhaldanos
comments.body: I find chatgpt valuable in discussing trading concepts, research, reference, synthesizing, help in some coding, validation of ideas, suggestions for improvements. It definitely saves time and effort and offers some help, but the deep creative/original process comes from oneself.

comments.author: dombleu
comments.body: They are discussion bots. They do not have any information that you can already access yourself, nor do they have any special insight. They code fast though.

comments.author: Single_Yesterday_433
comments.body: Don't waste time, study and read

comments.author: Reefersutherland21
comments.body: Do the work there is no ai that is going to save studying and discipline

comments.author: Fit_Ad2385
comments.body: For traders who are not very good at coding, ai chatbot definitely can help both coding and learning (in coding), although the code made by ai cannot be fully trusted

replies.author: Spirited_Syllabub488
replies.body: cannot be fully trusted unless properly analyzed and error tested.

replies.author: Nashmurlan
replies.body: Sorry for the noob question, but what are you actually coding? I thought we're talking about trading?

replies.author: Fit_Ad2385
replies.body: I’m coding Python algo trading, like SPX options, stocks and forex

replies.author: ID_N01
replies.body:
lol noobs like us have no use for that kind of thing yet, i mean at least to me. Getting a solid understanding of everything seems like the important first step. If we could just type a prompt, generate a code and money printer go brrr everyone would be rich.   
obv this is a dead comment but it seemed important to reply with this for any future me's doing the same foot work.   
Unless of course someone else thinks its a useful tool for a lvl 0 beginner ig?

comments.author: Uugly2
comments.body: I use Perplexity. I've used both of those models. Grok is very good. chatGPT is very good. I find them in a tie, but Grok is more concise. I have custom prompts that I use. Perplexity allows me to use them freely with whatever model. My preference has bounced around depending on my mood

comments.author: ResilientRootz
comments.body: chatgpts last update was somewhere around sept 2024 so as long as you remind it to pull current info it usually does fine for me, and normally and the first attempt at a real answer its like its pre-programmed to push hyped retail memes at you the 2nd or 3rd try usually has some good answers, i personally think chatgpt5 is 10k xs worse than chatgpt4o tho

comments.author: jenak5
comments.body: I use ChatGPT for breaking down backtesting data. It helps create a plan when the time of day and day of the week is most profitable for my strategy. It tells me what days are best to avoid.

comments.author: Finnext-AI
comments.body: In our app we actually got Grok and OpenAI model to team up as buddies. It’s pretty cool - instead of choosing one, we just let them work together and the combo feels super powerful.

comments.author: nuhmu
comments.body:
https://chatgpt.com/g/g-5wVuKfpEt-stocks-crypto-options-forex-market-summary

Try my free custom gpt if you want!

comments.author: Silly-Monitor-8583
comments.body:
ChatGPT currently has the best capabilities to help traders. You have the ability to set up a workspace that can analyze Pre Market, Post Market, your psychology/behavior, and coach you on what you suck at. 

You can literally build out a full workspace. I did this for a trader and analyzed 2 years of trades. 

Found he was exiting early on 59% of his trades, he over traded when he lost early in the morning, and he would lose trades outside of 10am-10:30am. 

Was very interesting to analyze the behavior and actual stats to put them to together. 

If you want to see the actual rules we enacted to help him just DM me

comments.author: Complete-Onion-4755
comments.body: I used perplexity for research because i think its the best for search.

comments.author: TrueSubstance196
comments.body:
This is why we are launching the AI Trading Performance Coach as it is NOT an alert or prediction tool. 

"The AI trading performance coach is designed for traders who want to improve their performance. It analyzes your trading history to identify your personal strengths and weaknesses, offering data-driven insights and personalized feedback to help you trade with more discipline and precision.This isn't a robo-advisor or a simple alert tool. It's an AI trading performance coach that analyzes your trades and personality to find hidden leaks in your execution and mindset. It learns your unique style over time, providing personalized insights and suggestions"

comments.author: InstanceWonderful735
comments.body: I have found Chatgpt excellent for Analysis and testing my Pie designs but i have also found Grox excellent at predicting market change and helping undrestand when im in a recovery stage after a crash and when i swap my tilt factoring weights. So i think both are excellent.

comments.author: NeighborhoodSalt9673
comments.body:
I am a little late to this post but wanted to share my insight for beginners who are visiting this thread.
For starters, who want to get familiar with options trading but also automate it as best as possible.
(This is all free, but I do pay for chat gpt)

Tell Chat GPT you want the work flow of a professional options trader.
It’ll have you set up screeners on Finviz to scan for stocks that could breakout, and anything else you ask it for.
You will run these scanners, upload screenshots to chat gpt and have it analyze them.
This is important so that we keep chat gpt using real time data.
Chat GPT will analyze these and probably direct you to Trading View where it will have you set alerts on promising stocks with potential breakouts for that day.
You can then upload screenshots of that current stock to chat GPT for it to analyze exactly when and when not to buy.
I sometimes upload a screenshot every two minutes to stay on track.

Keep in mind, if you’re like me, you’re limited on day trades. So this is just a good way to get familiar with the process and only leverage $50-$300 at a time.

I’ve used this for options buying for a week now and my scanners and chat gpt have worked almost flawlessly so far. I’ve been able to double or triple my money in about 10 minutes a few times.
Again, right now I’m not spending more than $250 on an option at a time and sometimes only spend $20 to test it out.

Also keep in mind, some options are expensive, so I have chat gpt look for contracts that I can afford right now.

I highly recommend this approach for beginners.
Don’t get greedy, take a profit of $5 if you need to, just to get comfortable with the process and your own emotional restraint.

Later, you can get a lot fancier.
